methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quobly is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Maud Vinet, Tristan Meunier, and François Perruchot. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies across public and private markets: 3 publicly traded peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private companies including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this case. The quantum computing peer set, while containing 5 same-sector companies, yields insufficient comparable data when filtered to same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier subsets. No grouping produced the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological bias given the heterogeneity in maturity, capital deployment, and technical approach across quantum platforms.

Without viable peer-median results, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range based on the data provided. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed fundraising history for Quobly, the wide dispersion in valuations and business models across the broader quantum computing ecosystem, and the nascent state of comparable seed-stage financing in this sector. A defensible valuation would require either (1) access to Quobly's specific financial metrics and technical milestones, or (2) expansion of comparable data through later-stage private market transactions or pilot revenue metrics.
